title: Crispy Australian Macadamia Tart
description: A sweet, flaky keto-friendly tart bursting with nutty macadamia flavor and a splash of Grand Marnier for a monsoon Australian twist.

ingredients.name: almond flour
ingredients.notes: provides a nutty base for a flaky crust

ingredients.name: butter
ingredients.notes: binds the crust and adds richness

ingredients.name: egg
ingredients.notes: acts as a binder in the crust

ingredients.name: macadamia nuts, chopped
ingredients.notes: the star ingredient, adds texture and flavor

ingredients.name: egg
ingredients.notes: binds the filling and creates a custard base

ingredients.name: Grand Marnier
ingredients.notes: used to deglaze and add citrusy depth

ingredients.name: coconut oil
ingredients.notes: used for sautéing nuts before deglazing

instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F) and grease a 9-inch tart pan.
Combine almond flour, coconut flour, sea salt, and erythritol in a bowl.
Add melted butter and the egg; mix until a dough forms.
Press the dough evenly into the prepared pan and bake for 10 minutes until lightly golden.
While the crust bakes, heat a skillet over medium heat and add the coconut oil.
Add the chopped macadamia nuts and toast for about 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Deglaze the skillet with Grand Marnier, swirling to lift the nut bits; let the alcohol evaporate for 1 minute.
In a separate bowl, whisk the heavy cream, two eggs, and vanilla extract until smooth.
Pour the wet mixture over the toasted nuts and stir to combine.
Fill the pre-baked crust with the filling mixture and bake for 25–30 minutes until set and edges are golden.
Remove from oven, let the tart cool for 15 minutes, then refrigerate for 30 minutes to firm up before slicing.
